name: Quails on Mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the duxelles, chop 6 oz of mushrooms and the stalks from the big flat mushrooms on which the quails will sit. Chop finely using a knife; do not use a food processor.
2. Mix in 1 very finely chopped garlic clove, about 9 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley, 3 tablespoons snipped chives, and 1 heaped tablespoon fresh tarragon (or 1½ teaspoons dried tarragon if fresh is unavailable).
3. Cook the mixture in a little butter over moderate heat for 3 minutes, stirring often. Season well with salt, pepper, and about 2 teaspoons lemon juice.
4. Let the duxelles cool before using it to stuff the quails. Spread it gently between the flesh and skin all over the breast area to plump up the quails.
5. Secure each bird's neck skin under itself with a wooden toothpick. If the skin is torn, put the stuffing into the body cavities instead.
6. Tie up each bird's legs to keep it neatly shaped.
7. Make about 5 oz of garlic butter and reserve it.
8. Choose a roasting tin or baking dish large enough to fit the 6 flat mushrooms side by side.
9. When ready to cook, melt a nugget of the garlic butter in the roasting tin. Turn the birds in it to coat them with fat, then arrange them, breast down, on a rack suspended across the roasting tin.
10. Bake at 425°F (220°C) gas mark 7 for 15 minutes.
11. Put the mushrooms in the tin, arranging them gill side up, and dot them with the remaining garlic butter. Return the birds to the top of the tin, breast side up, placing each one above a mushroom so that meat juices drip onto the mushrooms during cooking. Bake for an additional 20 minutes or until both birds and mushrooms are perfectly cooked.
12. Serve immediately or keep hot at 250°F (120°C) gas mark 1/2 until ready to serve. Rice, couscous, or bulgur and crisp peppery watercress pair well with this dish.
